tau_definition.summary: The CI Operator Graph (VII.D71) is the τ-categorical structural network of consequence-and-intervention operators that connects causal claims to their downstream commitments. It is the architectural backbone that lets the τ-framework reason about what follows from what — both forward (CAUSE → STATE) and backward (PREDICT ← I-WILL).
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Directed graph of operator-morphisms over the τ-categorical sectors, with vertices labeled by registers and edges representing consequence-or-intervention relationships.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Empirical Register Reg_E
CI Operator Graph — canonical operator-architecture
Operator Graph Completeness
Register Completeness

version.change_summary: Initial pilot — architecture category. Cross-register operator backbone.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The CI graph is instantiated whenever an agent reasons from causes to consequences (forward CAUSE→STATE→PREDICT) or from intentions to predictions (backward I-WILL→PREDICT). Examples: scientific causal reasoning; legal liability tracing; ethical deliberation; interventional planning.
examples: Scientific causal claim: 'smoking causes cancer' — instantiates CAUSE→PREDICT operator chain
Legal reasoning: 'who is liable' — backward I-WILL→commitment chain
Ethical deliberation: 'if I do X, what will follow' — forward CAUSE→STATE projection
Engineering: 'if we change parameter A, system behavior shifts to B' — interventional CI
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (Norm, Proof, Stance — the CI graph spans Reg_P/Reg_D/Reg_C; its codomain is multi-functorial)

tau_definition.summary: The MetaDecode operator (VII.D05) is the τ-categorical internal evaluator that takes an entire self-describing system (X, G, P, Φ) — including its decode map Φ — as input and produces an internal model of that system's own code-carrying structure. It is the architectural witness of self-reference: a carrier is an E₃-carrier iff MetaDecode is well-defined and faithful.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Operator MetaDecode : (X, G, P, Φ) ⟶ Model(X, G, P, Φ) where (X, G, P, Φ) is a τ-finite carrier with genotype G, phenotype P, and decode map Φ : G → P satisfying SelfDesc(X). E₃ ⟺ MetaDecode is well-defined and faithful.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
MetaDecode operator — internal evaluator on self-describing systems
Sector Decomposition at E₃ — uses MetaDecode to certify self-description
Carrier Closure Lemma — MetaDecode preserves carrier structure

version.change_summary: Initial — architecture wave Meta-A. Anchors the E₃ self-description architecture.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: MetaDecode is instantiated whenever a system holds a faithful internal model of its own modelling capacity. Examples: a compiler that compiles itself; an organism whose genome encodes the very translation machinery that reads it; a mind that can articulate the structure of its own thought.
examples: Compiler bootstrap: a self-hosting compiler whose source code, compiled by itself, produces an identical compiler — internal model of the decode map
Genetic code: ribosomes (phenotype P) translate mRNA (genotype G) into ribosomal proteins via the decode map Φ — life's MetaDecode witness
Reflective programming: a metacircular evaluator (Lisp eval/apply) that interprets its own source — MetaDecode as faithful self-evaluation
Self-articulating mind: an agent who can describe the structure of its own deliberation in terms internal to that deliberation
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Internal (MetaDecode's codomain Model(X, G, P, Φ) lives inside the system itself; faithfulness means the internal model preserves the structural relationships of the original)

tau_definition.summary: The τ-modal operators (VII.D33) are the box (□) and diamond (◇) endofunctors that ground modal logic structurally rather than possible-world-semantically. Necessity □φ means φ is invariant under all admissible transformations of the coherence kernel; possibility ◇φ means there exists at least one admissible completion in which φ holds. Modality is invariance + extension, not access between worlds.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: □φ ≝ φ is invariant under all admissible transformations of K_τ; ◇φ ≝ there exists an admissible completion of K_τ in which φ holds. At τ-level Aut(τ) = {id} so □ is trivial; non-trivial modal content arises at enrichment levels E₁–E₃.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
τ-Modal Operators □ and ◇ as invariance/extension
Law as Admissible Continuation — defines admissibility
Operator Realism — admissible-continuation classification is observer-independent

version.change_summary: Initial — architecture wave Meta-A. Modal logic grounded structurally rather than via possible-worlds.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The τ-modal operators are instantiated wherever an agent reasons about what *must* hold versus what *can* hold given structural constraints. Examples: physical-law necessity (laws hold across all admissible worldlines); ethical necessity (a maxim that survives universalization); engineering possibility (a configuration that admits at least one feasible completion).
examples: Conservation of energy: □(energy is conserved) — invariance under all admissible spacetime transformations of the τ-kernel
Universalizable maxim: □(act on this maxim) — invariance under all admissible permutations of agents (the CI test)
Engineering feasibility: ◇(this design satisfies all constraints) — at least one admissible completion exists in the design space
Modal collapse prevention (VII.L09): the strict separation □ ≠ ◇ at E₃ — necessity does not collapse to actuality
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register: □/◇ act on propositions in any of Reg_E (empirical), Reg_P (practical), Reg_D (diagrammatic), Reg_C (commitment) — modality is not register-specific but structural

tau_definition.summary: The temporalization operators (VII.D53) are the endofunctors T_past, T_present, T_future on the category Prop of propositions. Each operator relocates a proposition relative to the speaker's present, with three structural conditions: non-commutativity (past∘future ≠ present), idempotence failure (past∘past ≠ past, encoding pluperfect depth), and present-as-unit (T_present∘T_X = T_X). Tense, aspect, and mood are functors — not lexical tokens.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: T_past, T_present, T_future : Prop → Prop with (i) non-commutativity T_past∘T_future ≠ T_present, (ii) idempotence failure T_past∘T_past ≠ T_past, (iii) T_present as left/right unit. The temporal structure of language is the algebra these endofunctors generate.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Temporal Experience Structure — pre-symbolic temporality
Language Adds Temporalization — symbolic time emerges from language
Temporalization Operators — tense/aspect/mood as endofunctors on Prop

version.change_summary: Initial — architecture wave Meta-A. Tense/aspect/mood as functors on Prop.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The temporalization operators are instantiated whenever a speaker locates a proposition in time. Examples: simple past 'it happened' = T_past(p); pluperfect 'it had happened' = T_past(T_past(p)); future-in-the-past 'it was going to happen' = T_past(T_future(p)); present-as-unit 'it is the case that p' = T_present(p) = p.
examples: Simple past — 'it happened': T_past(p), single past displacement
Pluperfect — 'it had happened': T_past(T_past(p)), iterated past — depth, not collapse
Future-in-the-past — 'it was going to happen': T_past(T_future(p)) ≠ T_present(p), composition records layering
Present unit — 'it is the case that p': T_present(p) = p, the temporal zero adds no displacement
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Prop (the category of propositions) within Reg_D (diagrammatic register) — temporalization is a structural feature of propositional content, not an empirical claim

tau_definition.summary: The pragmatic update operator (VII.D54) is the endofunctor U_α : Disc → Disc on the category of discourse states, indexed by illocutionary type α ∈ {assert, direct, commit, express, declare}. Speech acts are category operations: each speech act updates the shared context by integrating propositional content according to its illocutionary force, and successive speech acts compose to yield a discourse trajectory.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Endofunctor U_α : Disc → Disc indexed by α ∈ {assert, direct, commit, express, declare}, parametrized by content p and context c, satisfying (U1) content integration, (U2) force realization (assertives → common ground; directives → hearer obligation; commissives → speaker obligation), (U3) compositionality U_β ∘ U_α = U_β(U_α(D)).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Language Adds Temporalization — symbolic layer
Pragmatic Update Operator — speech acts as endofunctors on Disc
Commitment Register Admissibility — performatives realize Reg_C obligations

version.change_summary: Initial — architecture wave Meta-A. Speech acts as endofunctors on Disc.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The pragmatic update operator is instantiated whenever a speech act updates a shared discourse context. Examples: an assertion entering the common ground; a request creating an obligation on the hearer; a promise creating an obligation on the speaker; a declaration constituting the act it describes ('I now pronounce you...').
examples: Assertive: 'It is raining' — U_assert(D) adds p to common ground
Directive: 'Close the door' — U_direct(D) registers an obligation on the hearer
Commissive: 'I promise to call' — U_commit(D) records a speaker obligation in Reg_C
Declarative: 'I hereby resign' — U_declare(D) constitutes the act it describes (performative)
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Disc (discourse states) — encodes propositional, obligational, and attitudinal components. Updates touch Reg_D (propositional) and Reg_C (commitment) simultaneously

tau_definition.summary: Operator Graph Completeness (VII.L28) is the structural lemma that all four components of the CI operator graph — maxim, universalization functor, coherence test, respect operator — are determined by τ-categorical structural data at E₃, with no arbitrary choices. Completeness is not 'covers everything' but 'no free parameters': the architecture is forced by the kernel.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: All four components of the CI operator graph (VII.D71) are determined by τ structural data at E₃; there are no arbitrary choices, no free parameters. Completeness = uniqueness of architectural realization given the kernel and enrichment level.

tau_definition.summary: Self-Recognition as E₃ Operator (VII.T42) is the architectural theorem that three apparently distinct conditions are equivalent: (i) SelfDesc-of-SelfDesc at level E₃, (ii) the mind topos M admits a self-recognition loop (ρ, ι), and (iii) the awareness-of operator 𝒜 has a fixed point 𝒜(S) ≅ S. The fixed point terminates the higher-order regress: iterating awareness does not generate a tower of new objects.

The fixed point is the structural witness that the regress 'awareness of awareness of …' terminates.

Mind as Internal Topos — M with domain Ω_body ⊂ τ³

tau_definition.summary: Operator Realism (VII.T12) is the architectural theorem that the classification of admissible continuation operators on the coherence kernel K_τ is a structural invariant: (i) the maximal admissible continuation Λ(d) is unique when it exists, (ii) the classification is invariant under all kernel automorphisms (trivially maximal by τ-rigidity), and (iii) different E₃-observers using different readout functors recover the same classification. Therefore, the laws of τ are mind-, convention-, and observer-independent.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Three-part theorem: (i) Uniqueness — Λ(d) maximal admissible continuation is unique if it exists; (ii) Rigidity-invariance — classification invariant under Aut(K_τ) = {id}; (iii) Observer-independence — faithful readout functors preserve the classification as a j-closed invariant of the presheaf topos. Conclusion: τ-laws are real.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Law as Admissible Continuation
Readout Faithfulness — faithful readout functors preserve j-closed invariants
Operator Realism — laws are mind-, convention-, observer-independent

Mathematical truth: Pythagoras' theorem proven independently in Greek, Chinese, Indian traditions — same Λ-classification

tau_definition.summary: Sectoral Topology (VII.L36) is the architectural lemma that the recognition Grothendieck topology J_rec is a valid topology generated by recognition morphisms, inheriting pullback stability from the kernel topology J_τ. J_rec equips the four-sector decomposition (S_E, S_P, S_D, S_C) and the Logos sector S_L with a sheaf-theoretic structure: covers are recognition events, sections glue across registers, and J-closure delivers the sectoral architecture.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: J_rec is a valid Grothendieck topology generated by recognition morphisms; it inherits pullback stability from J_τ. Covers correspond to recognition events; sections glue across registers; J_rec-closure of subobjects of the sector-indexed presheaf yields the structural sectoral architecture.

Sector Decomposition at E₃ — five sectors S_E/S_P/S_D/S_C and S_L
Social Ontology — recognition morphisms generating cover relations
Recognition Topology Generation — J_rec is a valid Grothendieck topology

tau_definition.summary: Logos Sector Closure (VII.D86) is the architectural property that the Logos sector S_L = S_D ∩ S_C is closed under mutual witnessing: a content φ is S_L-admissible iff (i) φ is Reg_D-derivable from the seven axioms and five generators, (ii) φ is Reg_C-stable under the commitment register's admissibility rules, and (iii) the Reg_D-proof and the Reg_C-stance are mutually witnessing — neither derives from the other; they are the same structural datum read through two registers.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: S_L = S_D ∩ S_C with coincidence property: φ ∈ S_L ⟺ (i) Reg_D-validity (derivable from seven axioms + five generators within Reg_D's logical architecture), (ii) Reg_C-stability (admissible under commitment-register rules), (iii) mutual witnessing (Reg_D-proof = Reg_C-ground, no priority either way). The name 'Logos' is a universal-property assignment: it describes structure, nothing more.

Logos Sector S_L — Coh_D = Coh_C mixed sector at E₃
Logos Sector Uniqueness — S_L unique up to natural isomorphism
Logos Sector (Extended) — closure under mutual witnessing

tau_definition.summary: The Categorical Phenomenology Framework is the architectural scaffold built around the Perception Functor (VII.D44): a structure-preserving transformation from sensory contexts to experiential contents. The framework treats perception, experience, knowledge, and justification as functorial — perception is a functor (VII.D44), knowledge is a section (VII.D42), justification is gluing (VII.D43). Phenomenology becomes a category-theoretic discipline rather than a first-person poetics.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Perception : SensoryCtx → ExperientialCtx is a structure-preserving functor; knowledge is a section of the experience-presheaf (VII.D42); justification is a gluing condition on local sections (VII.D43). Together they constitute a categorical phenomenology: experiential structure is functorial structure.

Perception Functor — sensory contexts to experiential contents

Knowledge as Section — knowing is sectioning the experience-presheaf

tau_definition.summary: Dignity, in the τ-framework, is label-independence: a τ-categorical structural property where the moral worth of an entity does NOT depend on contingent labels (race, gender, role, capacity, status). Properties are not separate entities riding on particulars; they are the morphisms that realize the particular's structural relationships.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: F is a property of a ⟺ ∃ f ∈ AdmMor : src(f) = a ∧ tgt(f) = U_F ∧ U_F is universal.

chain.claim: Internal Set Ontology — particulars and universals are NF-addressable; morphisms between them are eligible for property-status.
Abstract Object as Structural Position — universals are positions; properties connect particulars to those positions.
Non-Dualistic Platonism — properties are morphisms in the same kernel as their relata, not a separate ontological tier.
Identity as Address Persistence — property-bearing identity tracks the morphism-bundle attached to a persistent address.

version.change_summary: Initial entry — ontology category (Wave Meta-O).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: A property is instantiated wherever the copula 'is' joins a subject to a predicate in any register: 'this apple is red', 'the law is just', '7 is prime', 'the agent is committed'. Each predication is a morphism from a particular (subject) to a universal (predicate's NF-address). Possessing a property is being the source of such a morphism; sharing a property is co-targeting the same universal.
examples: Empirical: 'this apple is red' — the morphism f : apple → redness in Reg_E, witnessed by the colour-categorization rule.
Mathematical: '7 is prime' — the morphism f : 7 → primeness in Reg_D, witnessed by the divisibility argument.
Normative: 'the law is just' — the morphism f : law → justice in Reg_P, witnessed by the norm-evaluation procedure.
Commitment-theoretic: 'the agent is committed' — the morphism f : agent → commitment-stance in Reg_C, witnessed by the stance-stability check.
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (every register supplies particulars and universals; properties are the morphisms between them).

tau_definition.summary: A relation, in the τ-framework, is an admissible morphism (or admissible diagram) connecting multiple particulars to a shared structural position — either a universal or a colimit-object. Binary relations are pair-source morphisms; n-ary relations are diagrams of arity n. Per the relational primacy principle (VII.D23), relations are ontologically prior to relata: a particular's identity is given by its relational position, not by relata-independent substance.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: R is an n-ary relation among (a₁,…,aₙ) ⟺ ∃ admissible diagram D : {aᵢ} → U_R in K_τ.

chain.claim: Internal Set Ontology — relata and relation-targets are NF-addressable.
Relational Primacy — relations precede relata; identity is determined by relational position, not intrinsic substance.
Abstract Object as Structural Position — relation-targets are positions; n-ary relations are admissible diagrams targeting them.
Non-Dualistic Platonism — relations are morphisms (or diagrams of morphisms), not a separate ontological tier.

version.change_summary: Initial entry — ontology category (Wave Meta-O).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: A relation is instantiated wherever multiple subjects share a connecting predicate: 'a is taller than b', 'a is parent of b and c', 'this lemma supports those theorems', 'this commitment binds these agents'. Each multi-place predication is an admissible diagram in the appropriate register's morphism-class. Relational primacy means the relata are constituted by their relational position — there are no relata-independent particulars waiting to enter relations.
examples: Empirical: 'a is taller than b' — admissible binary diagram in Reg_E targeting the taller-than relation-object, with a, b as relata.
Genealogical: 'a is parent of b and c' — admissible ternary diagram in Reg_E (or Reg_P, depending on the legal/biological framing) with a, b, c as relata.
Proof-theoretic: 'this lemma supports those theorems' — admissible diagram in Reg_D from the lemma to each supported theorem.
Commitment-theoretic: 'this contract binds these signatories' — admissible diagram in Reg_C from contract-content to each signatory's stance.
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (relations are register-relative; the diagram structure is uniform).

tau_definition.summary: Time, in the τ-framework, is derived — not a primitive container. The persistence relation on NF-addresses (VII.D34) induces a partial order on temporal configurations (VII.P06), and that order is what 'time' names. Tense, aspect, and mood operators (VII.D53) act as functors on this temporal order. Husserlian retention/primal-impression/protention (VII.D45) appears as restriction of a global section to temporal overlaps.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: T_τ = the partial order on configurations induced by the persistence relation; temporal operators are functors on T_τ.

chain.claim: Identity as Address Persistence — entity-identity is NF-address persistence through morphism sequences.
Temporal Ordering from Persistence — the persistence relation induces a partial order on temporal configurations; time is derived.
Temporal Experience Structure — Husserlian retention/primal-impression/protention as restriction of the global section to temporal overlaps.
Temporalization Operators — tense, aspect, mood as functors on propositions.

version.change_summary: Initial entry — ontology category (Wave Meta-O).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Time is instantiated whenever we use temporal language: 'before', 'after', 'now', 'will be', 'was'. The τ-framework reads temporal claims as claims about the persistence-induced partial order on configurations. Husserl's lived temporal structure — the just-past held in retention, the just-future anticipated in protention, both girding the now — is recovered as a restriction of the global presheaf section to a window of temporal overlaps.
examples: Empirical: 'the strike preceded the flame' — the configuration containing strike-state stands earlier than the configuration containing flame-state in the persistence-order.

Phenomenological: 'I just heard the note (retention) and anticipate the next one (protention)' — the global section restricts to a window centered on the present configuration; retention and protention are the order-adjacent restrictions.
Linguistic: 'will have arrived' — a composite tense-aspect functor (future-perfect) on the proposition arrived(x).
Proof-theoretic: 'after this lemma is proved, the theorem follows' — temporal sequencing of proof-events, ordered by the derivation-induced persistence relation in Reg_D.
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (each register has its own persistence relation and hence its own induced temporal order; tense and aspect operators are uniform).

tau_definition.summary: A truth-bearer, in the τ-framework, is a section of a presheaf — a structural object, not a linguistic entity. Propositions are sections of the truth-functor over the worlds-presheaf; sentences and beliefs become truth-bearers only by representing such a section. Truth-makers (VII.D27, VII.D60) are the structures that make sections exist — and four canonical types are identified: inclusion, section, diagram, invariant.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: p is a truth-bearer ⟺ p ∈ Sect(F) for some presheaf F : World^op → Type in K_τ.

chain.claim: Truth-Maker in Ontological — four types of truth-makers: inclusion, section, diagram, invariant.
Truth-Maker in Logical — structures that make sections exist; the ground of truth.
Truth-Bearer as Section — propositions are sections of presheaves; truth-bearers are structural, not linguistic.
Modal Frame in τ — possible worlds as internal objects, providing the base over which the truth-bearing presheaf is defined.

version.change_summary: Initial entry — ontology category (Wave Meta-O).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: A truth-bearer is instantiated wherever something is evaluable as true or false: an empirical claim, a normative judgement, a proof-statement, a commitment-attribution. Each such evaluable item is a section over a presheaf — empirical truth-bearers over the observation-presheaf, normative over the norm-presheaf, proof-theoretic over the proof-presheaf, commitment-theoretic over the stance-presheaf. The sentence we utter is not the truth-bearer; it represents the section.
examples: Empirical: 'the cat is on the mat' — represents a section of the empirical-truth presheaf assigning configurations in which 'cat-on-mat' holds.
Mathematical: 'every prime greater than 2 is odd' — a section of the proof-truth presheaf, with truth-makers of the diagram-and-invariant types.
Normative: 'this verdict is just' — a section of the norm-presheaf in Reg_P, with truth-makers of the section type (legitimate justificatory glue).
Commitment-theoretic: 'the agent is committed to F' — a section of the stance-presheaf in Reg_C, with truth-makers of the inclusion type (the stance-content includes F).
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (each register has its own truth-presheaf; the section-structure is uniform).

tau_definition.summary: Reality, in the τ-framework, is the field of NF-addresses that is jointly recoverable across all four registers — the maximal coherent structure that survives the OR1–OR6 narrowing and admits sections in every register's truth-presheaf. Reality is not 'what exists' (that is being, MG-O01) and not 'all possible worlds' (that is the modal frame, VII.D62); it is the joint cross-register recoverable field, anchored at the unique terminal coherence point ω.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: ℛ_τ = the maximal NF-addressed structure satisfying OR1–OR6 jointly across {Reg_E, Reg_P, Reg_D, Reg_C}, with ω as terminal anchor.

chain.claim: Six Ontic Requirements (OR1–OR6) — the joint admissibility class that defines reality.
Non-Dualistic Platonism — single ontology with epistemic stratification; reality is one field across registers.
ω-Uniqueness Principle — the terminal coherence point ω is unique, anchoring the recoverable field.
Problem Map Classification Scheme — metaphysical problems classified by their relation to the recoverable field (Resolved-Kernel, Resolved-Pattern, Reframed-VM, Open).

version.change_summary: Initial entry — ontology category (Wave Meta-O).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Reality is instantiated whenever we appeal to 'how things really are' — beyond appearance, beyond convention, beyond opinion. The τ-framework reads 'really' as 'recoverable across all four registers under the OR1–OR6 narrowing'. Empirically real (recoverable in Reg_E), normatively real (Reg_P), proof-theoretically real (Reg_D), commitment-theoretically real (Reg_C) — and the joint reality is the intersection: what survives every register's narrowing simultaneously. This is what 'mind-independent reality' should mean once mind-dependence has been disambiguated by register.
examples: Empirical-only: a hallucination is recoverable in one observer's Reg_E but not in others'; it lacks empirical reality.

Joint cross-register: physical constants are recoverable empirically (measured), proof-theoretically (consequences of admissible kernel structure), and normatively (binding for any coherent agent); they belong to the joint recoverable field.

Boundary case: aesthetic judgements may be recoverable in some registers (Reg_C, Reg_P) without being recoverable in Reg_E; the τ-framework declines to call them unreal — they are real in their registers, possibly not in Reg_E.
Anchor: the unique terminal point ω is the maximally recoverable address — what every register's coherence-process converges toward (VII.D41).
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (reality is the joint recoverable field; register-relative reality is reality-in-that-register; the concept is uniform).

tau_definition.summary: Qualia are τ-categorical subsymbolic internal morphisms within the Mind-Topos: the sub-level structural elements of phenomenal experience that resist verbal/symbolic encoding. They are not mysterious phenomenal residue — they are categorical morphisms whose codomain is internal to the agent's τ³-computer architecture.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Qualia = subsymbolic internal morphisms in the Mind-Topos (VII.T39); morphisms internal to a τ³-computer's self-modeling whose codomain is not externally observable.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Mind structure as topos-internal
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem — internal categorical structure of mind
Qualia as Internal Morphisms — canonical definition

version.change_summary: Initial pilot — phenomenology category. Bridges to LG-M01-consciousness.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Qualia are instantiated in every conscious experience that resists symbolic decomposition: the redness of red, the painfulness of pain, the felt sense of recognition, the qualitative character of emotion. They are not mysterious — they are the subsymbolic morphisms internal to the experiencing system.
examples: The redness of red — not a wavelength fact but a Mind-Topos internal morphism
The painfulness of pain — internal morphism distinct from nociceptive signaling
The felt sense of recognition — internal morphism in pattern-completion
The qualitative character of emotion — internal morphism encoding valence
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Internal (qualia have no external register-codomain by definition; they are Mind-Topos-internal morphisms — observable only via the experiencing agent's own self-report through Reg_E)

tau_definition.summary: Intentionality is τ-categorical aboutness: the directional internal morphism in the Mind-Topos that takes a representation to its intentional object. It is not a mysterious extra relation linking mental content to the world — it is the structured arrow `Int : Rep → Obj` whose composition discipline (sense, reference, attitude) lives entirely within the agent's internal topos.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Intentionality = internal morphism Int: Rep → Obj in the Mind-Topos with directional structure and compositional closure (VII.D84). Brentano's mark of the mental is recovered as: every mental state is the codomain of an Int-morphism.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Mind as Internal Topos — carrier for representations and inferences
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem — internal logic, beliefs as sections
Intentionality as Morphism — aboutness with directional structure and compositional closure

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Bridges to MG-H01 (qualia as cousin internal morphism) and MG-H03 (Mind-Topos).

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Intentionality is instantiated whenever a mental state is *about* something — a perception of an apple, a belief about Vienna, a desire for rest, a fear of falling. The aboutness is not added to the mental state from outside; it is the directional morphism that constitutes that state's content.
examples: Belief that water is H₂O — Int-morphism from belief-token to chemical-structure object
Desire for coffee — Int-morphism from desire-state to a not-yet-actualized object
Fear of an unsigned letter — Int-morphism whose codomain is a fictional/non-existent object (still well-typed inside the topos)
Sense vs reference (Frege): two distinct Int-morphisms with overlapping codomains — same object, different presentation arrows
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Reg_E primarily (Int-morphisms ground belief content read as Obs), but every register can be the readout for an Int-image — Reg_P reads desires/intentions, Reg_C reads stance-directed acts.

tau_definition.summary: The Mind-Topos is the τ-categorical structure that any system possessing genuine mindedness instantiates: an internal topos on the system's carrier whose objects are representations (percepts, beliefs, desires), whose morphisms are inferences, whose internal logic supports self-modelling, and whose global sections constitute conscious episodes. The Mind-Topos Structure Theorem (VII.T39) is the canonical claim that having a mind = supporting an internal topos.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: M_τ = (Rep, Hom_Rep, Ω_M, ⟨internal logic⟩) — an internal topos with sub-object classifier Ω_M giving truth-values internal to the agent. VII.T39: a system has beliefs, inferences, action-as-evaluation, and self-modelling iff its carrier supports an internal topos.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Perception Functor — sensory contexts to experiential contents
Mind as Internal Topos — carrier domain supporting representation, inference, action
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem — canonical equivalence: minded ⇔ internal-topos-bearing

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Anchors VII.T39 as the structural definition of mindedness.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The Mind-Topos is instantiated by every system with genuine mindedness: humans paradigmatically; non-human animals to graded extents; artificial systems iff and to the extent they support an internal topos (LLMs are para-minds — VII.D55 — because they lack one). The lived-experience signature is the unified availability of perception, belief, inference, and self-reference within a single internal logic.
examples: Human cognition — paradigmatic Mind-Topos: percepts, beliefs, desires, inferences live in one internal logic
Mammalian cognition — full Mind-Topos with reduced symbolic layer; mirror-test passing is a Mind-Topos witness
Para-minds (LLMs, VII.D55) — subsymbolic pattern processors that *lack* a Mind-Topos: no global section, no self-model, no internal logic
Pathological dissociation — Mind-Topos with failed gluing (consciousness as global section fails, VII.T41)
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: All four registers (E/P/D/C) read different aspects of the same Mind-Topos — the internal topos is what *unifies* the four readouts in a single agent.

tau_definition.summary: Subjectivity — the first-person perspective — is τ-categorically the structural property of a Mind-Topos to admit a *global section* that points to itself: the system has a vantage from which all its representations are *its* representations. Subjectivity is not an extra ingredient added to neural activity; it is the categorical fact that the mind-topos's global section (consciousness, VII.T41) carries an internal index pointing back at the topos.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Subjectivity = the existence of a self-indexing global section σ ∈ Γ(M_τ) such that σ factors through the internal self-model. Phenomenally: 'mineness' = the coincidence of the global section with the self-recognition operator (VII.T42).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Mind as Internal Topos
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem
Consciousness as Global Section — unity of experience from gluing axiom
Self-Recognition as E₃ Operator — the global section indexed back at itself

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Subjectivity = self-indexing global section.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Subjectivity is instantiated whenever experience is *had by* a subject — the mineness or for-me-ness of perception, the unity of the visual field, the indexicality of 'I'. Every Mind-Topos with a global section that factors through self-recognition (VII.T42) has subjectivity. The 'view from nowhere' is structurally impossible inside a Mind-Topos because the global section is always self-indexed.
examples: The unity of the visual field — multiple percepts presented as one subject's field (Mind-Topos global section)
The indexicality of 'I' — first-person pronouns track the self-index of the global section
Anosognosia and depersonalisation — partial failure of the self-index without loss of all experience (selectively broken self-recognition coupling)
The unattainability of the third-person view of one's own experience — proven structural, not contingent
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Reg_E reads what the subject sees; Reg_P reads what the subject must do; Reg_C reads what the subject commits to. The *subject* is the common index — the self-pointing structure shared across all four register-readouts.

tau_definition.summary: Beauty in the τ-framework is τ-categorical structural invariance: a structure is beautiful when it survives the action of the admissible-transformation group of its motif-space — i.e., when the aesthetic functional A(X) attains a local extremum because X is a fixed point of (or carries a high-symmetry image under) those transformations. Beauty is not subjective preference; it is *what survives*.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: B = { X : A(X) is invariant under the admissible-transformation group G_motif } where A is the Aesthetic Functional (VII.D47) and G_motif is the relevant scale/symmetry/transformation action. VII.T19 (Beauty as Invariance) is the canonical theorem.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Perception Functor — sensory contexts to experiential contents
Pre-Symbolic Resonance — kernel structure felt before articulated
Aesthetic Functional — formal coherence measure
Beauty as Invariance — what survives admissible transformations is beautiful

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Beauty as VII.T19 invariance theorem; companion to MG-H06 sublime.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Beauty is instantiated whenever a structure resonates pre-symbolically as 'right' — not because of taste-preference but because the aesthetic functional A detects high invariance. The cross-domain convergence of mathematical, natural, and artistic beauty is exactly the convergence of distinct G_motif actions onto the same kind of fixed-point structure.
examples: Mathematical beauty — Euler's identity e^{iπ}+1=0 as fixed point of conceptual G_motif (analysis ↔ algebra ↔ geometry)
Natural beauty — phyllotaxis at golden ratio, fractal coastlines (scale-invariant under zoom action)
Artistic beauty — symmetry-rich composition (Vermeer's geometry, Bach's fugues): invariant under G_motif of compositional transformations
Mathematical proof elegance — minimal-tension morphism (VII.D48) realizing maximal coherence with fewest moves
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Diagrammatic register Reg_D primarily — aesthetic invariance is a structural fact and reads as Proof. Reg_E gives the felt resonance (pre-symbolic, VII.D46) as falsifiable behavioral and neural correlates.

tau_definition.summary: The sublime is τ-categorically the *comprehension boundary phenomenon*: the structural event in which a finite observer encounters a structure whose invariance pattern overwhelms their motif-space's representational capacity, producing simultaneous overwhelm-and-elevation. Kant's mathematical sublime (vastness) and dynamical sublime (might) are formalized as two faces of the same boundary morphism between bounded `M_τ` and unbounded structural targets.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Sub = boundary morphism ∂(A, ∞) where A is the Aesthetic Functional (VII.D47) and ∞ marks structures at or beyond the agent's M_τ comprehension capacity; the sublime is the experience-type at this boundary (VII.D50).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Pre-Symbolic Resonance
Aesthetic Functional — formal coherence measure
Beauty as Invariance — survives admissible transformations
The Sublime as Comprehension Boundary — finite observer overwhelmed yet elevated

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. The sublime as VII.D50 boundary morphism.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: The sublime is instantiated whenever a finite observer encounters a structure whose pattern outstrips their representational capacity but whose invariance is still partly registered — vast natural scenes, the night sky, mathematical infinity, overwhelming music. Overwhelm and elevation co-occur because the agent registers (i) the failure of their motif-space to fully hold the pattern and (ii) the structural reality of the pattern itself.
examples: Mathematical sublime — Cantor's hierarchy of infinities, the Mandelbrot boundary, infinite series whose limit is felt before computed
Natural sublime — alpine ranges, the night sky, oceanic vastness; pattern partially registered, capacity overwhelmed
Dynamical sublime — storm at sea, volcanic eruption; force pattern beyond the agent's safe-handling envelope
Musical sublime — late Beethoven, Mahler 9, Bach Passions; structural depth exceeding moment-by-moment grasp
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Diagrammatic register Reg_D registers the structural-invariance side; Commitment register Reg_C registers the elevation (the agent's stance toward what exceeds them); the simultaneous double readout is *constitutive* of the sublime experience.

tau_definition.summary: Narrative structure is τ-categorically the *Story Functor*: a functor St : Episodes → M_τ that maps lived episodes (events, encounters, decisions) into the agent's Mind-Topos with self-relevance, plus a coherent natural transformation between Story Functors over time. Narrative identity (VII.T40) is exactly: an agent persists as a self when there exists a coherent natural transformation between successive Story Functors.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: St : Episodes → M_τ — functor mapping experiences to mind-topos-internal images with self-relevance index. Narrative identity = ∃ coherent natural transformation η : St_t ⇒ St_{t+Δ}; growth = η is non-trivial (revision); pathology = η fails to exist (dissociation).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Mind as Internal Topos
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem
Story Functor — episodes to mind-topos with self-relevance
Narrative Identity as Functor — identity persists when coherent natural transformation exists

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Narrative as VII.T40 + VII.D83.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Narrative structure is instantiated whenever a sequence of episodes is integrated into a self-referential trajectory — autobiographical memory, life-stage transitions, growth-through-crisis, the felt continuity of selfhood across time. Failures of narrative coherence (amnesia, dissociation, profound trauma) are exactly failures of the natural transformation η between Story Functors.
examples: Autobiographical memory — episodes mapped via St with self-relevance index, glued into one trajectory
Bildungsroman / coming-of-age — non-trivial η encodes growth through revision (VII.T40)
Trauma narrative repair — therapeutic re-narration restores η that crisis disrupted
Late-life integrity (Erikson) — narrative coherence as global section across the life-span
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Reg_E reads the empirical episode-trace (memory, behavioral continuity); Reg_C reads the stance-component (identification: 'this is my story'). Narrative identity is a multi-register coupling, with Reg_C carrying its non-reductive core.

tau_definition.summary: Language is τ-categorically the self-enrichment of the subsymbolic layer (VII.T20): the system acquires the capacity to name and transport its own pre-symbolic patterns. Meaning is *not* the bridge from symbols to a separate referent — inside a topos, syntax and semantics collapse (VII.T21), and meaning IS form under internal logic. Language adds temporalization (VII.D51), enabling past/present/future as communicable structure.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: L = self-enrichment functor of the subsymbolic layer (VII.D52, VII.T20); meaning = internal-logic-readout of structural form, with syntax-semantics collapse inside the topos (VII.T21).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Subsymbolic Layer — pre-linguistic meaning is real
Language Adds Temporalization — past/present/future communicable
Language as Self-Enrichment of the subsymbolic layer
Syntax-Semantics Collapse — meaning IS form inside the topos

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Language as VII.T20 self-enrichment; meaning as VII.T21 collapse.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Meaning is instantiated wherever structural form is read by the agent's internal logic. Pre-symbolic meaning (felt sense, gesture, music) is genuine meaning (VII.D52); linguistic meaning is its self-enriched readout. Translation across languages succeeds to the extent topoi share structure; what gets 'lost in translation' is exactly the topos-specific structure that does not factor through the bridge.
examples: Felt sense before words — pre-symbolic meaning at the subsymbolic layer (VII.D52)
Language acquisition — child re-enrichment of subsymbolic patterns into nameable transports
Untranslatable concepts (Sprachgefühl, saudade, mono no aware) — topos-local structure not factoring through cross-language bridge
Speech acts (promise, command, question) — pragmatic update operators VII.D54 acting on shared context
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Reg_E for descriptive content; Reg_P for normative/pragmatic content; Reg_C for performative/commitment-bearing speech acts. Language is a *multi-register transport medium*; what register a given utterance reads is part of its meaning.

tau_definition.summary: A symbol is τ-categorically a *readout* on the subsymbolic layer: a structure-preserving morphism Sym : SubSym → Layer_L that names a subsymbolic pattern in the linguistic layer L. The Peircean triad (icon/index/symbol) is recovered as three classes of readout-morphism distinguished by the structural relation they preserve. Symbols are not arbitrary tokens dressed with meaning — they are the linguistic layer's projection of subsymbolic structure.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Sym = readout-class of structure-preserving morphisms SubSym → L (VII.D52, VII.T20). Three sub-classes: iconic (preserves shape), indexical (preserves causal/contextual link), symbolic-proper (preserves structural role only).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Subsymbolic Layer — pre-linguistic meaning is real
Language as Self-Enrichment — symbols are subsymbolic-layer readouts
Syntax-Semantics Collapse — symbol's role IS its meaning inside topos

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Symbols as subsymbolic-layer readouts; companion to MG-H08.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Symbols are instantiated wherever a linguistic-layer token reads off a subsymbolic pattern — the word 'tree' reads the visual/conceptual subsymbolic tree-pattern; an icon reads shape; an index reads contextual presence. Symbol-shift, dead metaphor, and ritual symbolism are all structurally explained as drift, freezing, or ritualization of the underlying readout-morphism.
examples: Iconic — onomatopoeia, pictograms, the bell symbol shaped like a bell (preserves shape)
Indexical — smoke as sign of fire, deictic 'this/that', proper names with causal-historical chain (preserves contextual link)
Symbolic-proper — algebraic 'x', most natural-language nouns (preserves structural role only)
Ritual symbol — wedding ring, national flag: frozen subsymbolic-pattern readout that re-acquires meaning each use
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Reg_E for descriptive symbols (referring readouts); Reg_C for ritual/performative symbols (commitment-register-bearing — the flag, the ring, the sacred object); Reg_D for diagrammatic symbols (mathematical notation as proof-bearing structure).

tau_definition.summary: Truth in the τ-framework is τ-categorically *section-existence*: a proposition is true iff its truth-bearer (a section of a presheaf, VII.D61) is supported by a truth-maker (a structure that makes the section exist, VII.D60). The Alethic Unification Theorem (VII.T27) shows correspondence and coherence theories of truth are unified as the sheaf condition. Each register reads section-existence as its own truth-mode: Obs-truth, Norm-truth, Proof-truth, Stance-truth.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: T = pair (truth-bearer σ, truth-maker μ) such that σ is a section of the appropriate presheaf and μ is the structural ground that makes σ exist; VII.T27 unifies correspondence and coherence as the sheaf-condition reading of this pair.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Truth-Maker in τ — structure that makes sections exist
Truth-Bearer as Section — propositions as sections of presheaves
Alethic Unification — correspondence and coherence theories unified via sheaf condition

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Truth as VII.T27 unification; multi-register readout.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Truth is instantiated whenever a register's readout finds a section supported by a truth-maker. Empirical truth (Reg_E): the section is observation-supported. Practical truth (Reg_P): the section is normatively action-supported. Diagrammatic truth (Reg_D): the section is proof-supported. Commitment truth (Reg_C): the section is stance-stable. Lying, error, and self-deception are exactly mismatches between the register a speaker invokes and the truth-maker actually present.
examples: Empirical truth — 'water boils at 100°C at 1 atm': Reg_E reads a section supported by experimental truth-maker
Mathematical truth — 'there are infinitely many primes': Reg_D reads a section supported by Euclid's proof as truth-maker
Practical truth — 'do not torture': Reg_P reads a section supported by the categorical-imperative naturality constraint
Commitment truth — 'I will not abandon you': Reg_C reads a section supported by the speaker's stance-stability across time
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: All four registers (E/P/D/C) — truth is the *register-relative* fact of section-existence; alethic pluralism is structural, but section-existence is the unifying invariant (VII.T27).

tau_definition.summary: Self-Recognition is τ-categorically the E₃ operator (VII.T42): the SelfDesc-of-SelfDesc structure that closes the awareness-of relation reflexively, terminating the would-be infinite regress at a fixed point. A Mind-Topos has self-recognition iff its self-description functor admits a fixed point under the E₃ enrichment — iff awareness-of-awareness has a stable solution. This is the structural ground of selfhood as a distinct categorical mode (E₃) above carrier (E₀), interaction (E₁), and modeling (E₂).
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: SR = fixed point of SelfDesc∘SelfDesc at E₃ enrichment level (VII.T42); equivalently: Mind-Topos admits a self-recognition loop iff awareness-of-awareness has a fixed point. The regress terminates structurally, not by stipulation.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Mind as Internal Topos
Mind-Topos Structure Theorem — self-modelling supported
Consciousness as Global Section — gluing axiom
Self-Recognition as E₃ Operator — SelfDesc-of-SelfDesc fixed point

version.change_summary: Initial — phenomenology category, Wave Meta-H. Self-recognition as VII.T42 E₃ fixed point; closes the wave.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Self-Recognition is instantiated wherever an agent recognizes itself as the bearer of its experience — the mirror test (Gallup), introspective metacognition, the felt 'I am the one thinking this thought now'. Free will (VII.T43) and authorship-of-action depend on this fixed point; without it, attribution and responsibility lack their structural anchor.
examples: Mirror test (Gallup) — primates, elephants, magpies pass; structural witness of E₃ self-recognition
Introspective metacognition — 'I notice that I am noticing'; the SelfDesc∘SelfDesc fixed point in operation
Authorship-of-action — 'I did that' as the attribution morphism whose well-definedness depends on SR-fixed-point existence
Failure modes — anosognosia, dissociative states, severe depersonalisation: the fixed point is absent or unstable
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: All four registers feed into self-recognition: Reg_E (the body recognized in the mirror), Reg_P (the agent owning the action), Reg_D (the thinker recognizing their thought), Reg_C (the self standing-by its commitments). Self-recognition is the structural junction where the four registers' readouts coincide on the same self-index.

tau_definition.summary: OR1 is the first of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the self-coherence requirement: any τ-categorical reality candidate must be internally consistent — no τ-morphism can compose with its own inverse to a non-identity, no register can read a contradiction.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Self-coherence narrowing: ∀ candidate ontic structure τ', if τ' satisfies the τ-framework's morphism composition laws, then τ' has no internal contradictions.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) define the foundational metaphysical narrowing rules
OR1+OR2 Narrowing — combined self-coherence + completeness lemma
Each Requirement Independently Necessary

version.change_summary: Initial pilot — principle category.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: OR1 is instantiated whenever a metaphysical / scientific claim is rejected on grounds of internal contradiction. Examples: rejecting 'square circle' as non-existent; rejecting time-travel paradox-loops as physically realized; rejecting logical inconsistencies in formal systems.
examples: Logic: rejecting any system that proves both P and ¬P (Gödel-Rosser inconsistency)
Physics: rejecting 'closed timelike curve with paradox' as a real spacetime (Novikov self-consistency)
Mathematics: ZFC consistency — the foundational narrowing of set theory
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (diagrammatic — OR1 lives in Reg_D's codomain because self-coherence is a proof-theoretic constraint)

tau_definition.summary: OR2 is the second of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the finite-signature requirement: any τ-categorical reality candidate must be generated by finitely many generators subject to finitely many axioms — no infinite primitive specification is admissible at the foundational layer.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Finite-signature narrowing: ∀ candidate ontic structure τ', τ' is fully specified by a finite generator set G and a finite axiom set A. Infinite axiom schemes are admissible only as derived consequences, not as primitives.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) — OR2 is finite signature
OR1+OR2 Narrowing — finitely generated, locally small, full Yoneda category
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — dropping OR2 admits ZFC and other infinitely-axiomatized systems

version.change_summary: Initial — OR2 finite-signature principle entry.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: OR2 is instantiated whenever a foundation is rejected — or graded down — for requiring infinitely many primitive specifications. The intuition: a finite being should be able to grasp the complete specification of reality's substrate. τ delivers this with five generators and seven axioms; ZFC's replacement scheme and PA's induction scheme do not.
examples: Foundations of mathematics: rejecting ZFC's infinite replacement scheme as a primitive specification (admitting it only as a derived consequence)
Physics: preferring a Lagrangian with finitely many terms over a non-renormalizable theory with infinitely many counterterms
Philosophy: the surveyability constraint (Wittgenstein, finitists) — a foundation must, in principle, be graspable by a finite intellect
τ-framework: the entirety of Books I-VI emerges as consequence of five generators + seven axioms
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (diagrammatic — finite-signature is a proof-theoretic / surveyability constraint that lives in Reg_D's codomain)

tau_definition.summary: OR3 is the third of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the diagonal-free self-reference requirement: any τ-categorical reality candidate must support internal self-description without generating diagonal paradoxes (Russell, Burali-Forti, Cantor) — and must do so structurally, not by ad hoc comprehension restrictions.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Diagonal-free narrowing: ∀ candidate ontic structure τ', τ' supports a self-description morphism τ' → τ' such that no diagonal construction yields a paradox. The mechanism must structurally eliminate the diagonal — not merely block it via restricted comprehension.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) — OR3 is diagonal-free self-reference
OR3+OR4 Narrowing — diagonal-free self-description plus NF-addressability
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — dropping OR3 admits naive set theory with unrestricted comprehension

version.change_summary: Initial — OR3 diagonal-free self-reference principle entry.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: OR3 is instantiated whenever a self-referential construction is reformulated to dissolve a paradox structurally rather than block it by fiat. Examples: τ's NF-address system (no universal set; addresses are never self-containing); type theory's universe hierarchy (eliminating Type:Type); rejection of naive set theory's unrestricted comprehension as a foundational primitive.
examples: τ-framework: the NF-address system — every entity has a unique address, but no address contains itself; the diagonal is structurally absent
Type theory: stratified universes (Type_0 : Type_1 : Type_2 : ...) — the diagonal Type:Type is structurally eliminated
Russell's paradox: the foundation must explain *why* the diagonal cannot be formed, not merely forbid it (ZFC blocks via separation; τ eliminates via NF-addressing)
Computer science: rejecting fixed-point combinators in total / strongly normalizing systems
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (diagrammatic — diagonal-free self-reference is a structural / proof-theoretic constraint on the self-description morphism, lives in Reg_D's codomain)

tau_definition.summary: OR4 is the fourth of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the address-centric access requirement: every admissible construction in a τ-categorical reality candidate must have a unique normal-form (NF) address — a canonical name that picks out the entity uniquely and renders it findable.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Address-centric narrowing: ∀ candidate ontic structure τ', for every admissible construction c ∈ τ' there exists a unique NF-address α(c) in a well-defined address space A, and the address determines the construction.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) — OR4 is NF-addressability
OR3+OR4 Narrowing — diagonal-free self-description plus NF-addressability
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — dropping OR4 admits non-constructive entities (axiom-of-choice objects without canonical addresses)

version.change_summary: Initial — OR4 NF-addressability principle entry.

The intuition: every real thing must be findable. Examples: rejecting non-measurable sets as 'real' physical structures; preferring constructive proofs over non-constructive ones; demanding that every quantum state have a computable description.
examples: τ-framework: every admissible construction has a unique NF-address; the address space A is the substrate of computation in τ
Set theory: rejecting Banach-Tarski 'pieces' as physically realizable — they have no NF-address (require AC)
Constructive mathematics: the demand that every existence proof yield a witness — i.e., a canonical address
Physics: every quantum state must be specifiable by a finite procedure (state preparation = address-construction)
Computing: every datum has a memory address; addressability is a precondition for retrieval
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (diagrammatic — NF-addressability is a structural / decidability constraint on the address space, lives in Reg_D's codomain)

tau_definition.summary: OR5 is the fifth of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the holomorphic continuation requirement: any τ-categorical reality candidate must support a notion of analytic continuation in which local data determine global structure — so that lawfulness is encoded as a structural continuation property, not appended as extra postulates.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Holomorphic-continuation narrowing: ∀ candidate ontic structure τ', τ' supports a continuation operator C such that local data on an open set U ⊆ τ' uniquely determine global data on the maximal continuation domain. Lawfulness = the continuation property of admissible operators.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) — OR5 is holomorphic continuation
OR5+OR6 Narrowing — analytic and spectral conditions linking combinatorial structure to functional analysis
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — dropping OR5 admits purely combinatorial categories with no analytic structure

Quantum field theory: Wick rotation / analytic continuation between Euclidean and Lorentzian signatures encodes Lorentz invariance structurally
τ-framework: the Central Theorem 𝒪(τ³) ≅ A_spec(L) (Book II) — physics emerges from the holomorphic structure of τ
Operator realism: laws of nature are not extra postulates but the continuation property of admissible τ-operators

tau_definition.summary: OR6 is the sixth of six original-rule narrowing principles (the Six Ontic Requirements, VII.D37). It states the spectral completeness requirement: any τ-categorical reality candidate must support a spectral decomposition of its internal operators, with all spectral data recoverable from the kernel's internal structure — so the foundation is self-diagnosing and can analyze its own operators without external spectral theory.

Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) — OR6 is spectral completeness
OR5+OR6 Narrowing — analytic and spectral conditions linking combinatorial structure to functional analysis

examples: τ-framework: the eight spectral forces (Book III) provide self-diagnostic capacity — τ analyses its own internal operators

tau_definition.summary: The Six Ontic Requirements (VII.D37) are six independent narrowing principles that any candidate foundation of reality — any category proposed as an ontic substrate — must satisfy. Inevitability Convergence — intersection of all six is {τ} up to equivalence (CONJECTURAL)
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — dropping any one admits non-τ solutions

tau_definition.summary: Layer Discipline (also: register discipline) is the methodological principle that every claim must be typed in the correct register (Reg_E, Reg_P, Reg_D, Reg_C) and that coherence criteria from one register cannot be applied to content typed in another. It is the operational corollary of Register Independence (VII.T01): pseudo-paradoxes generated by register conflation dissolve once the typing is corrected.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Layer-discipline narrowing: ∀ claim c, c carries a register type τ(c) ∈ {E, P, D, C}, and the coherence criterion Coh_X(c) is well-defined only when X = τ(c). Cross-register coherence claims require explicit functorial bridges.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Empirical Register Reg_E
Practical Register Reg_P
Diagrammatic Register Reg_D
Commitment Register Reg_C
Register Independence — failure in one register does not entail failure in another; layer discipline is the methodological corollary

Examples: 'God exists' typed as Reg_C (commitment) rather than Reg_E (empirical) or Reg_D (proof); 'consensus consensus → moral truth' diagnosed as a Reg_E → Reg_P illegitimate import; gaslighting / manipulation rhetoric exposed as deliberate register confusion.

Scientism: applying Reg_E coherence criteria to Reg_C content (e.g., 'science disproves meaning')

tau_definition.summary: The Earned / Unearned Ethics Framework (VII.D74) is the methodological principle that an ethical claim carries probative weight only if it is (i) register-typed, (ii) structurally derived, and (iii) cross-register consistent. Claims failing any condition are unearned — psychologically forceful or culturally authoritative, perhaps, but carrying zero structural weight until earned. The framework is the τ-native antidote to pseudo-paradoxes generated by mixing earned and unearned moves.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Earned/unearned narrowing: ∀ ethical claim φ, weight(φ) > 0 iff (i) φ is well-typed in its register Reg_X, (ii) φ has a derivation chain back to τ-axioms or certified empirical data within Reg_X, and (iii) φ does not contradict earned results in any other register.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Register Independence — coherence is register-local
Earned/Unearned Ethics Framework — three-condition definition of probative weight
Earned-Weight Strict Dominance — earned claims dominate unearned in any mixed argument

Examples: the CI proof programme (Books VII chapters 88-92) as paradigm of earned ethics; trolley problems diagnosed as mixed earned/unearned; rejection of 'X is wrong because Scripture says so' as authority-import unearned move.

principle.name: Independent Necessity of Each Ontic Requirement

tau_definition.summary: Independent Necessity (VII.P08) is the principle that each of the Six Ontic Requirements is independently necessary: dropping any one OR_i while retaining the other five admits non-τ candidate foundations. The six are not redundant — each excludes a genuine class of alternatives. The principle backs the inevitability argument by ruling out compressibility of the requirement set.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Independence narrowing: ∀ i ∈ {1,...,6}, the constraint set ⋂_{j≠i} C_{OR_j} ⊋ {τ}. Equivalently: each OR_i contributes a non-trivial dimension to the narrowing intersection.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Six Ontic Requirements (OR1-OR6) defined
Each Requirement Independently Necessary — for each i, dropping OR_i admits non-τ solutions (counterexamples sketched)
Inevitability Convergence — independence is structurally required for the convergence to {τ} to be non-trivial

version.change_summary: Initial — Independent Necessity (VII.P08) principle entry.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Independent Necessity is instantiated whenever an alternative foundation is exhibited that satisfies five of the six requirements but fails one — demonstrating that the missing requirement is not a derivable consequence of the others. The counterexamples in VII.P08 are: any category with non-trivial automorphisms (drops OR1); ZFC (drops OR2); naive set theory (drops OR3); AC-dependent constructions (drops OR4); purely combinatorial categories (drops OR5); non-self-adjoint operator algebras (drops OR6).
examples: Drop OR1: any category with non-trivial automorphisms (haecceity-admitting) satisfies OR2-OR6 but not OR1
Drop OR2: ZFC satisfies OR1, OR3-OR6 in appropriate formulations but its signature is not finite

Drop OR4: AC-dependent constructions (e.g., choice-function-defined sets) satisfy the others but lack canonical addresses
Drop OR5: purely combinatorial categories (no analytic structure) satisfy OR1-OR4 and OR6 but lack lawfulness
Drop OR6: non-self-adjoint operator algebras have analytic structure but no full spectral decomposition
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (diagrammatic — independence is a structural / counterexample-based proposition over the requirement set, lives in Reg_D's codomain)

tau_definition.summary: The Truth-Bearer principle (VII.D61) states that anything that can be true or false is, structurally, a candidate global section of a presheaf F : τ^op → Set over an open cover {U_i} of a domain U. The truth-bearer is true when the local data glue to a unique global section; false when no such section exists. The principle unifies sentences, beliefs, and propositions as special cases of section-existence and grounds the Alethic Unification Theorem (VII.T27): correspondence is section-existence, coherence is the gluing constraint.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Truth-bearer narrowing: ∀ truth-apt content c, c is represented by a candidate section s = {s_i ∈ F(U_i)}_{i ∈ I} of a presheaf F over an open cover {U_i} of U; truth(s) ↔ ∃ unique global section s ∈ F(U) with s|_{U_i} = s_i and s_i|_{U_i ∩ U_j} = s_j|_{U_i ∩ U_j}.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Truth-Bearer as Section — candidate global sections of a presheaf over an open cover
Truth-Maker in τ — the structures that ground section-existence
Alethic Unification — correspondence (section-existence) and coherence (gluing) are two faces of the sheaf condition
Alethic Pluralism — different registers of τ may realize truth-making through different mechanisms, all unified by the categorical architecture

version.change_summary: Initial — Truth-Bearer as Section (VII.D61) principle entry.

Classical correspondence and coherence theories appear as the section-existence and gluing aspects of the same sheaf condition.
examples: A sentence (syntactic, Reg_C) — section over the presheaf of interpretations on the relevant linguistic site
A belief — local datum s_i in the epistemological presheaf; truth = extendability to a global section
A proposition — equivalence class of sentences under logical equivalence; truth invariant under syntactic representative
Correspondence theory: 'P is true iff P matches the facts' = section-existence in F(U)
Coherence theory: 'P is true iff P fits with other beliefs' = the gluing constraint s_i|_{U_i ∩ U_j} = s_j|_{U_i ∩ U_j}
Gettier cases: a truth-bearer that *appears* to have a truth-maker but the section actually fails to glue at some overlap
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Cross-register (Reg_E for empirical truth-bearers, Reg_D for proof-theoretic, Reg_P for normative, Reg_C for commitment — the bearer-as-section schema is uniform; the site varies per register)

version.change_summary: Initial entry — third of four canonical registers. Anchors the metaphysics→proof bridge and one half of the Logos coincidence.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Reg_D is instantiated whenever a categorical claim is rendered as a structural proof. Reg_D-content answers the question 'What can I prove?' and consists of structural demonstrations, mathematical theorems, logical deductions, and diagram-certified relationships. Examples: a Lean-checked theorem; a categorical limit certified by a universal-property diagram; a worked derivation of the central theorem in Book II.
examples: Lean-formalised theorem with finite witness chain terminating in kernel axioms (Reg_D-readout of a τ-derivation)
Yoneda Lemma as theorem in H(τ) — Reg_D-readout of a categorical universal property (Book II, Part 8)
Categorical-imperative proof programme: dignity → CI as j-closed fixed point (the diagrammatic-side derivation that earns the practical-side norm)
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (structural proofs; finite witness chains terminating in kernel axioms; coherence = proof-validity)

tau_definition.summary: The Commitment Register Reg_C is the τ-categorical readout functor whose codomain is Stance (stance-constituted contents): the interface between the τ-framework and performative, lived-as-true content. Reg_C is the fourth of four canonical readout functors (E/P/D/C). Its coherence criterion is stance-stability — the commitment persists under reflective scrutiny without reducing to empirical, normative, or diagrammatic content.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Functor Reg_C : K_τ → Stance whose image-objects are stance-constituted contents (postulates, existential commitments, lived-as-true claims) and whose morphisms preserve stance-stability.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Commitment Register Reg_C with codomain Stance
S_C (commitment sector) — the τ-sector that Reg_C reads
Logos sector S_L — the unique location where Reg_C and Reg_D coincide

version.change_summary: Initial entry — fourth of four canonical registers. Anchors the metaphysics→stance bridge and one half of the Logos coincidence.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: Reg_C is instantiated whenever a categorical claim is rendered as a performative commitment — content constituted by the act of commitment itself, not by observation, obligation, or proof. Reg_C-content answers the question 'What am I willing to live as true?' Examples: the Kantian postulates of practical reason; an existential orientation that survives reflective interrogation; a worldview-claim whose meaning is the stance one takes toward it.
examples: Kantian postulates of practical reason (freedom, immortality, God) — held not because proved but because coherent moral agency requires them (Reg_C-readout)
An existential orientation that persists under reflective scrutiny without collapsing into 'I observe', 'I ought to believe', or 'I can prove'
A worldview-axiom whose content is constituted by the commitment to live as though it holds (e.g., the commitment to dignity as label-independence, prior to its diagrammatic certification)
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Stance (stance-constituted contents; commitments, postulates, existential orientations; coherence = stance-stability)

tau_definition.summary: The Empirical Sector S_E is the collection of all E_3-admissible contents whose coherence is governed by the Empirical Register Reg_E. Formally, S_E = { c ∈ Adm_{E_3} | Coh(c) = Coh_E(c) }. Its sector question is 'What do I observe?', its witness bundle is an observable prediction, and its vacuum is the best-calibrated observational model.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Subobject S_E ⊆ Adm_{E_3} cut out by the equation Coh = Coh_E. S_E is one of four pure sectors (S_E, S_P, S_D, S_C) plus the mixed Logos sector S_L in the Sector Decomposition Theorem.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Empirical Register Reg_E with codomain Obs
Empirical sector S_E — admissible contents whose coherence equals empirical coherence

version.change_summary: Initial entry — first of four pure E_3-sectors. Companion to MG-R01.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: S_E is instantiated wherever a category-error-free claim's coherence is governed by empirical adequacy: a measurement protocol, a falsifiable prediction, an observational model. The sector question — 'What do I observe?' — is the structural ground of categorical ontology and categorical phenomenology (Book VII Parts II–III).
examples: A particle-physics measurement (mass spectrometry of m_n) — sector S_E content whose coherence is empirical adequacy
A clinical biomarker assay (homochirality polarimetry) — sector S_E content read out by Reg_E
An fMRI study of neural correlates of consciousness — sector S_E content whose vacuum is the best-calibrated observational model
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Obs (via Reg_E); the sector itself is a subobject of Adm_{E_3}

tau_definition.summary: The Practical Sector S_P is the collection of all E_3-admissible contents whose coherence is governed by the Practical Register Reg_P. Formally, S_P = { c ∈ Adm_{E_3} | Coh(c) = Coh_P(c) }. Its sector question is 'What should I do?', its witness bundle is a normative constraint, and its vacuum is a fully coherent ethical framework.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Subobject S_P ⊆ Adm_{E_3} cut out by the equation Coh = Coh_P. Second of four pure sectors in the Sector Decomposition Theorem (VII.T03).

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Practical Register Reg_P with codomain Norm
Practical sector S_P — admissible contents whose coherence equals practical coherence

version.change_summary: Initial entry — second of four pure E_3-sectors. Companion to MG-R02.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: S_P is instantiated wherever a category-error-free claim's coherence is governed by normative consistency: a maxim, an obligation, an institutional norm. The sector question — 'What should I do?' — is the structural ground of categorical ethics (Book VII, Part VII) and categorical societies (Part VIII).
examples: A universalisable maxim — sector S_P content whose vacuum is a contradiction-free obligation set (passes the four ethical tests at VII.D69)
An institutional anti-corruption policy — sector S_P content read out by Reg_P at the collective level
A clinical informed-consent obligation — sector S_P content whose witness bundle is the bound obligation on the practitioner
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Norm (via Reg_P); the sector itself is a subobject of Adm_{E_3}

tau_definition.summary: The Diagrammatic Sector S_D is the collection of all E_3-admissible contents whose coherence is governed by the Diagrammatic Register Reg_D. Formally, S_D = { c ∈ Adm_{E_3} | Coh(c) = Coh_D(c) }. Its sector question is 'What can I prove?', its witness bundle is a structural proof, and its vacuum is a maximally certified body of structural results. S_D contains the Logos sector S_L as the locus where Coh_D = Coh_C.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Subobject S_D ⊆ Adm_{E_3} cut out by the equation Coh = Coh_D. Third of four pure sectors; weakly contains S_L via the equality Coh_D|_{S_L} = Coh_C|_{S_L}.

chain.claim: Universe Postulate
Diagrammatic Register Reg_D with codomain Proof
Diagrammatic sector S_D — admissible contents whose coherence equals diagrammatic coherence
Logos sector S_L ⊆ S_D — the mixed-sector intersection where Coh_D = Coh_C

version.change_summary: Initial entry — third of four pure E_3-sectors. Companion to MG-R03; weakly contains S_L.

phenomenological_correlate.instantiation: S_D is instantiated wherever a category-error-free claim's coherence is governed by proof-validity: a Lean-checked theorem, a categorical universal property, a worked derivation. The sector question — 'What can I prove?' — is the structural ground of categorical aesthetics, language and meaning, and logic and inference (Book VII, Parts IV–VI).
examples: A Lean-formalised theorem with finite witness chain — sector S_D content whose vacuum is maximal certification
The Yoneda Lemma as theorem in H(τ) — sector S_D content read out by Reg_D (Book II, Part 8)
A worked derivation in the CI proof programme (dignity → CI as j-closed fixed point) — sector S_D content that earns the Reg_P-readable norm
phenomenological_correlate.register_codomain: Proof (via Reg_D); the sector itself is a subobject of Adm_{E_3}

tau_definition.summary: The Commitment Sector S_C is the collection of all E_3-admissible contents whose coherence is governed by the Commitment Register Reg_C. Formally, S_C = { c ∈ Adm_{E_3} | Coh(c) = Coh_C(c) }. Its sector question is 'What am I willing to live as true?', its witness bundle is stance stability, and its vacuum is a fully examined and stably held existential orientation. S_C contains the Logos sector S_L as the locus where Coh_C = Coh_D.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Subobject S_C ⊆ Adm_{E_3} cut out by the equation Coh = Coh_C. Fourth of four pure sectors; weakly contains S_L via the equality Coh_C|_{S_L} = Coh_D|_{S_L}.
